{{Infobox group
| name = Twin Star Sect
| introduced = cradle-10-reaper
}}

''See also: [[Twin Star Sect Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Twin Star]] Sect is an expansive organization founded by a [[Sage]] to provide protection and training for its members. Known for its inclusive recruitment, the sect accepts individuals regardless of their specific [[Path]]. The organization is characterized by its distinctive two-colored star emblem, which appears on banners and robes in various color combinations such as pale blue with burnt orange or red, sky-blue with blood-red, and navy with dark scarlet. The sect maintains a significant presence in [[Serpent's Grave]], where its headquarters include specialized training halls and [[aura]] chambers. Leadership and instruction within the sect involve high-level practitioners, including [[Kelsa]], who provides guidance on the [[Path of the White Fox]], and [[Jai Long]], who trains in the [[Path of the Stellar Spear]]. The sect also utilizes advanced assets like the [[Heart of Twin Stars]] and specialized entities known as the Twin Guardians. While some view the organization as a source of security for those seeking comfort during turbulent times, others have expressed concerns, with some labeling it a cult due to its associations. Despite these perceptions, the sect operates with significant influence, overseeing territories and managing various members through a structured hierarchy of acolytes and trained practitioners.}}

== Reaper ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Twin Star Sect]] has a headquarters on the edge of Serpent’s [[Grave]].<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c11">''Reaper'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-10-reaper|The [[Twin Star Sect]] is a group [[Yerin]] asks about.<ref name="cradle-10-reaper-c24">''Reaper'', chapter 24</ref>}}

== Dreadgod ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Twin Star]] Sect is identified by their two-colored star banners that surround their city and members who wear light blue robes accented with orange and red. Those belonging to the sect typically practice either dream or light [[Paths]]. During a significant conflict, combat took place directly within the sect's compound. Following these events, [[Lindon]] relocated the entire organization.}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Twin Star Sect]] has two-colored star banners around the city.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c5">''Dreadgod'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The [[Twin Star Sect]] has light blue robes with orange and red highlights.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c8">''Dreadgod'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|Members of the [[Twin Star Sect]] practiced light or dream [[Paths]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c15">''Dreadgod'', chapter 15</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|The fight is occurring in the [[Twin Star Sect]] compound.<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c17">''Dreadgod'', chapter 17</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-11-dreadgod|[[Lindon]] relocated the [[Twin Star Sect]].<ref name="cradle-11-dreadgod-c21">''Dreadgod'', chapter 21</ref>}}

== Waybound ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Identified by their split star symbol and two-colored star banners, the [[Twin Star]] Sect has maintained a working relationship with the [[Hall]]. Despite these ties, certain individuals have accused the sect of being a [[Dreadgod cult]]. As their flags fly over [[Sacred Valley]], members of the sect have been known to spread legends regarding [[Jai Chen]]. Ultimately, the group gathered in the name of [[Lindon]] because many sought to claim the protection of the [[Void Sage]].}}

{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Twin Star Sect]] flies the two-colored star.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c5">''Waybound'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Hall]] has been working with the [[Twin Star Sect]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6">''Waybound'', chapter 6</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Some people are calling the [[Twin Star Sect]] a [[Dreadgod cult]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c6" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|Some students in the [[Twin Star Sect]] spread legends about [[Jai Chen]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32">''Waybound'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Twin Star Sect]] flags fly over [[Sacred Valley]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Twin Star Sect]]'s symbol is a split star.<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32" />}}
{{spoiler|book=cradle-12-waybound|The [[Twin Star Sect]] gathered in [[Lindon]]'s name because people wanted to borrow the protection of the [[Void Sage]].<ref name="cradle-12-waybound-c32" />}}

== Threshold: Stories from Cradle ==
{{spoiler|book=cradle-13-threshold|The [[Twin Star Sect]] uses blue-and-orange banners.<ref name="cradle-13-threshold-c8">''Threshold: Stories from Cradle'', chapter 8</ref>}}

== Notes ==
<references />

{{Cradle nav}}

[[Category:Groups]]
[[Category:Reaper]]
[[Category:Dreadgod]]
[[Category:Waybound]]
[[Category:Threshold: Stories from Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle]]
[[Category:Cradle groups]]
